title: Day 09 — Movie Search Application
summary: Optimize search with debouncing, extract custom hooks, type paginated APIs with generics, and build a movie search app with loading, empty, error, and pagination states.

blocks.content: Day 08 taught you to fetch data and handle UI states. Day 09 teaches you to fetch smartly. Every production search — Netflix, Amazon, Google — debounces keystrokes so the server is not hammered on every letter. You will build that optimization, extract it into a custom hook, and add pagination on top of yesterday's fetch patterns.

blocks.title: Week 2 Day 2
blocks.content: You already know fetch, useEffect, and controlled inputs from Week 1 and Day 08. Today adds debouncing and custom hooks. Do NOT introduce Context API (Day 12) or Redux (Week 3).

blocks.text: JavaScript — Debouncing, Timers, Event Loop Intro
Debouncing — wait until the user stops typing

blocks.content: Debouncing delays an action until input settles. Type 'batman' quickly — without debounce that is six API calls. With a 400ms debounce, one call fires 400ms after the last keystroke. This is how real search bars work.

setTimeout schedules a macrotask. clearTimeout cancels a pending timer. In React, every time the search term changes you must clear the previous timer in useEffect cleanup — otherwise stale timers fire with old values.

JavaScript runs one call stack at a time. setTimeout callbacks are macrotasks queued after the current stack clears. fetch Promises resolve as microtasks. Debounce timers sit in the macrotask queue — understanding this helps you explain why debounced search feels instant but API calls happen later. Deep dive on Day 11.
Use debounce for search-as-you-type, window resize handlers, and scroll position saves. Wait until activity stops.
Throttle fires at most once per interval — useful for scroll events. Search uses debounce, not throttle. Know the difference in interviews.

blocks.title: Do not fetch on every onChange
blocks.content: The number-one Day 09 bug: calling fetch inside onChange without debounce. Open Network tab — if you see six requests for six keystrokes, you forgot debouncing.
Paginated APIs share the same shape: results array, page number, total pages. Generics let you define PaginatedResponse<T> once and reuse it for movies, products, and users.

blocks.title: Production habit
blocks.content: Keep generic wrappers in src/types/api.ts. Feature-specific types like Movie live in src/types/movie.ts. Components import MovieSearchResponse — never inline generic shapes in JSX.

blocks.text: React — Custom Hooks and Search Optimization

blocks.content: Custom hooks extract reusable stateful logic. useDebounce takes a value and delay, returns the debounced value. Rules: name starts with use; can call other hooks; same rules as components.

Search term lives in useState. Input value={search} onChange updates search immediately for responsive UI. Fetch triggers on debouncedSearch — not on every keystroke. Reset page to 1 when debouncedSearch changes.

blocks.title: Separate input from fetch trigger
blocks.content: search drives the input. debouncedSearch drives the API. page drives pagination. Three related states — do not collapse them into one variable or pagination breaks.

items.text: Controlled search input with debounced API trigger
Empty state before search and when zero results

blocks.title: Hints
blocks.content: Sign up for a free TMDB API key; store it in .env as VITE_TMDB_API_KEY. Test debounce by typing quickly and watching Network tab — one request per pause, not per letter. Reset page when search changes.
Symptom: Network tab shows b, ba, bat requests. Cause: fetch in onChange without debounce. Fix: use useDebounce; fetch in useEffect when debouncedSearch changes.

Symptom: Old movie list flashes before new results. Cause: Slow response from previous query arrives after fast new query. Fix: AbortController or cancelled flag from Day 08; clear movies when query changes.
Symptom: Double fetch on page change or infinite loop. Cause: Unstable deps or fetch effect missing page or debouncedSearch. Fix: [debouncedSearch, page] with ESLint exhaustive-deps enabled.
